framework_name: MITRE Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) — 2024 Top 25 Sample

techniques.name: Cross-site Scripting
techniques.description: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

techniques.name: Out-of-bounds Write
techniques.description: The product writes data past the end, or before the beginning, of the intended buffer.

techniques.name: SQL Injection
techniques.description: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

techniques.name: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
techniques.description: The web application does not verify that a valid request from the user was intentionally provided.

techniques.name: Path Traversal
techniques.description: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

techniques.name: Out-of-bounds Read
techniques.description: The product reads data past the end, or before the beginning, of the intended buffer.

techniques.name: OS Command Injection
techniques.description: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

techniques.name: Use After Free
techniques.description: Referencing memory after it has been freed can cause a program to crash, use unexpected values, or execute code.

techniques.name: Missing Authorization
techniques.description: The product does not perform an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action.

techniques.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
techniques.description: The product allows the attacker to upload or transfer files of dangerous types.

techniques.name: Code Injection
techniques.description: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

techniques.name: Improper Input Validation
techniques.description: The product receives input or data, but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input has the properties that are required to process the data safely.

techniques.name: Command Injection
techniques.description: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')

techniques.name: Improper Authentication
techniques.description: When an actor claims to have a given identity, the product does not prove or insufficiently proves that the claim is correct.

techniques.name: Improper Privilege Management
techniques.description: The product does not properly assign, modify, track, or check privileges for an actor, creating an unintended sphere of control.

techniques.name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
techniques.description: The product deserializes untrusted data without sufficiently verifying that the resulting data will be valid.

techniques.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
techniques.description: The product exposes sensitive information to an actor that is not explicitly authorized to have access to that information.

techniques.name: Incorrect Authorization
techniques.description: The product performs an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action, but it does not correctly perform the check.

techniques.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
techniques.description: The web server receives a URL or similar request from an upstream component and retrieves the contents of this URL, but does not sufficiently ensure that the request is being sent to the expected destination.

techniques.name: Improper Restriction of Operations within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer
techniques.description: The product performs operations on a memory buffer, but it can read from or write to a memory location that is outside of the intended boundary of the buffer.

techniques.name: NULL Pointer Dereference
techniques.description: A NULL pointer dereference occurs when the application dereferences a pointer that it expects to be valid, but is NULL, typically causing a crash or exit.

techniques.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
techniques.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key, which it uses for its own inbound authentication, outbound communication to external components, or encryption of internal data.

techniques.name: Integer Overflow or Wraparound
techniques.description: The product performs a calculation that can produce an integer overflow or wraparound when the logic assumes that the resulting value will always be larger than the original value.

techniques.name: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
techniques.description: The product does not properly control the allocation and maintenance of a limited resource, thereby enabling an actor to influence the amount of resources consumed, eventually leading to the exhaustion of available resources.

techniques.name: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
techniques.description: The product does not perform any authentication for functionality that requires a provable user identity or consumes a significant amount of resources.
